title: calotropis procera subsp. hamiltonii
intro: Calotropis procera subsp. hamiltonii, also known as the Indian or Queen's crape myrtle, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can be utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Calotropis procera subsp. hamiltonii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant include extracts and infusions, which can be used as remedies for various ailments, and it is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
